food_subgroup: Specialty milk-based coffee and herbal latte drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry exists for butterfly pea flower or purple gold latte. FOODON_03301737 (latte beverage) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM (coffee-based beverages) used as closest parent. No specific code exists for butterfly pea flower latte or purple gold latte variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specialty beverage. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual café drink variants at this specificity.

allergens: possible oat (gluten) if oat milk variant used

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g (one cup or glass), as typically served in Malaysian and Singaporean café settings.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: whole milk (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #01077), espresso (USDA FoodData Central #14209), butterfly pea flower tea (negligible macros), turmeric powder (USDA FoodData Central #02043), and added sugar; weighted by typical recipe proportions for a 300 ml café latte with herbal infusion.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or USDA for Purple Gold Latte. Nutrient values estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 220 ml whole milk, 30 ml espresso, 5 g turmeric powder, 10 g added sugar, butterfly pea flower infusion (negligible macros). Per-100g values derived from this 300 g serving estimate. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk-based beverages and lattes; analogous estimate based on full-fat milk GI (27) and added sugar contribution, weighted for a café latte with approximately 7.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Purple Gold Latte. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous milk-based latte with added sugar. Full-fat milk GI is approximately 27 (University of Sydney GI Database); added sugar (sucrose) GI is 65. Weighted blend for this formulation (predominantly milk with moderate sugar addition) yields an estimated GI of approximately 45, classified as low (≤55). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (45 × 23.4 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 10.5. Butterfly pea flower and turmeric contribute negligible glycemic load.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~114 mg per 300 g serving) and low saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: Purple Gold Latte contains approximately 114 mg sodium per standard serving, which is modest; individuals with hypertension may consume it occasionally but should avoid adding extra sugar syrups or condensed milk, which can contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and moderate carbohydrate content (~23 g per 300 g serving) with estimated GI of 45
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated glycemic index of 45 and approximately 23 g of carbohydrate per serving (glycemic load ~10.5), Purple Gold Latte poses a moderate glycemic concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control and requesting reduced or no added sugar is advisable to limit postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat per serving; butterfly pea flower and turmeric provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory phytochemicals
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Purple Gold Latte provides approximately 4.5 g saturated fat per 300 g serving, which is within acceptable limits for cardiovascular health; the inclusion of butterfly pea flower anthocyanins and turmeric curcuminoids may confer modest anti-inflammatory benefits, though clinical evidence in beverage form remains limited.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Purple Gold Latte is a visually striking specialty café beverage originating from Malaysian and Singaporean coffee culture, popularised by artisan coffee bars such as EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street in Singapore. The drink derives its distinctive deep purple-to-violet colour from an infusion of butterfly pea flower (Clitoria ternatea), a Southeast Asian herb traditionally used in Nyonya and Malay cuisine for natural food colouring. The 'gold' element typically refers to the addition of turmeric, which imparts a warm golden hue and earthy, mildly spiced flavour note. The base is a steamed milk latte — usually whole milk or oat milk — combined with a shot of espresso or strong coffee, creating a layered visual effect where the purple butterfly pea infusion and the golden turmeric milk blend or contrast depending on preparation style. The flavour profile is mildly floral from the butterfly pea flower, subtly spiced from turmeric, creamy from the milk, and lightly sweet from added sugar or honey. Nutritionally, a standard 300 ml serving provides approximately 186 kcal, 7.2 g protein, 7.5 g fat, and 23.4 g carbohydrate, with meaningful calcium (~246 mg) and riboflavin from the milk base. The estimated glycemic index is 45 (low), making it a moderate glycemic beverage when consumed without excessive added sugar. Butterfly pea flower anthocyanins and turmeric curcuminoids contribute antioxidant and anti-inflammatory phytochemicals, though clinical evidence for health benefits in beverage quantities remains preliminary. The drink is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal. It is priced at SGD 9.00 at EC Coffee Bar and is representative of the premium specialty beverage trend in Singapore's café scene.

What gives the Purple Gold Latte its purple colour?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Purple Gold Latte exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis of constituent ingredients (whole milk, espresso, turmeric, butterfly pea flower infusion, added sugar) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as the primary source for individual components. GI is an analogous estimate based on milk-based latte with added sugar from the University of Sydney GI Database reference tables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for whole milk (#01077), espresso (#14209), turmeric powder (#02043), and sucrose, weighted for a typical 300 g café latte serving. No direct laboratory analysis or approved database entry exists for this specific product. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; this is confirmed zero by formulation — no hydrogenated fats are used in this beverage. 3. GI is an analogous estimate (GI=45, low) based on milk-based latte with moderate added sugar; documented in gi_source. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 0.7 from milk fat composition per USDA SR Legacy). 5. Butterfly pea flower anthocyanins and turmeric curcuminoids are bioactive phytochemicals present in trace-to-moderate amounts; their specific nutrient values are not separately enumerated in standard nutrient databases and are subsumed within the component estimates. 6. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as this is a liquid café drink. 7. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as this food is served in Singapore and the cuisine is classified as Malaysian, satisfying the Singaporean/Malaysian multilingual requirement. 8. All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not separately reported in the component-level USDA SR Legacy entries used for estimation and are not calculable from the available source data without laboratory analysis.
